Weekly Markets Monitor - Jail break or head fake?

PDF
2.03 mb

HighlightsA data light shortened week in the US put the spotlight on Fed Chairman Warsh’s first meeting and a perceived ‘hawkish pause’. Sentiment was however aided by signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the US and Iran. Eurozone data shone a little brighter, China’s growth data continued to surprise to the downside while higher inflation readings printed in Japan.Global equities rose alongside bond yields. The dollar index (DXY) followed suit. Oil slumped.The US dollar index (DXY), taking its cue from the Fed’s ‘hawkish pause’, higher bond yields across the curve and stronger equities – broke comfortably above 100 last week (C.O.T.W). Whether this marks beginning of a more sustained appreciation remains unclear. Factors that could label this a false break-out are: Softer-than-expected US PCE or ‘trimmed mean’ inflation prints; BoJ intervention to limit yen weakness; reduced need for dollar liquidity should oil start to flow again; and improving non-US growth. But if the trend does establish itself, it may exert further marginal pressure on gold prices.

Chart of the week: Jail break or head fake? 

Source: Bloomberg, World Gold Council
